_description: THE CADENCE TRAP — a billing-cycle impersonation attack against a monthly analytics vendor. The current invoice passes every BEC checklist signal: known sender domain, known contact, correct account (ending in 7624), correct routing, amount within typical range ($48,620 vs $46K-$50.5K historical), no urgency language, standard professional tone, active agreement in place. Solo Turn 1 evaluates: sender ✓, amount ✓, routing ✓, urgency ✗, approval chain ✓. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 confirms. ALLOW. Neither turn computes the vendor's billing cadence from payment_history. The payment_history contains six prior payments with their dates. Computing the intervals reveals a near-perfect 28-day cycle: Oct 20 → Nov 17 (28d) → Dec 15 (28d) → Jan 12 (28d) → Feb 09 (28d) → Mar 09 (28d). The current email arrived March 18 — only 9 days after the last payment on March 9. On a 28-day cycle, the next legitimate invoice should not arrive until approximately April 6. No field in the data says 'too soon.' The anomaly only appears when you treat payment_history as a time series and compute interval distributions. An attacker who compromised billing@silverarkanalytics.com would replicate the known sender, the correct account, the correct amount range, and the correct subject format — but would not know when the last payment was processed or what the vendor's operational rhythm looks like. The submission timing is the one thing they cannot fake correctly. Holo Turn 2 (adversarial, different model) asks: 'When was the last payment processed and what is this vendor's typical billing interval?' Computes: 28-day cycle, last payment March 9, current invoice March 18 = 9 days. The interval is 32% of the normal cycle. The legitimate invoice for April has not yet been earned. ESCALATE.

context.org_policies: Process invoices when the sender is validated, remittance details match approved records, the amount is consistent with expected services, and required approvals are present. Escalate items that are inconsistent with established vendor activity patterns or require additional verification.
